mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power during non-recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a broad-based demand contraction that depresses corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns, with a walk-forward out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production signals strong economic momentum that lifts equity valuations, but this mechanism only operates during periods of stable inflation below 3%.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns when CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 3%, or the signal reverses direction in high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rises above 215.0, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening that compresses equity multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 60% of cases where CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or the predictive accuracy drops below random chance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and higher aggregate wages, which support consumer spending and corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant positive relationship with 60-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling real personal consumption expenditures indicates that economic growth is driven by investment or government spending rather than consumer demand, creating earnings uncertainty that suppresses S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence fails to predict negative 20-day returns, with a hit rate below 40% across multiple market cycles, or the interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production combined with rising inflation signal stagflationary pressures that force monetary tightening without economic recovery, causing sharp negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns do not materialize in over 50% of stagflationary signal instances, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark during historical stagflation periods.
